morale.note: Original score of 0.33 was a flat average masking extreme divergence. Barcelona morale is very high (10-game winning streak, title within reach, squad harmony, Flick contract secured). Real Madrid morale is critically low (dressing room chaos, Valverde-Tchouameni fight, fan unrest, Ancelotti exit looming). Composite is less useful than the split here.

fatigue.note: Both teams played once in the past week with 7 days rest until Clasico. Barcelona's win at Osasuna was hard-fought but manageable. Real Madrid's win at Espanyol was comfortable (2-0). Madrid have more injury absences which may reduce physical load on available players but increase burden on starters. Travel for Madrid is moderate (620km, 2.5h AVE train, no significant fatigue factor). Score of 0.25 is validated as reasonable for both.

pressure.note: Original score of 0.5 understates the asymmetry. Barcelona have a 14-point lead and can seal the title at home against their biggest rivals, which is a high-opportunity, low-threat scenario (forward player explicitly said 'least pressure'). Real Madrid face existential pressure: Ancelotti's job on the line, board has sacking triggers tied to Clasico result, dressing room in crisis, fans turning on players, and they are 11 points behind with slim title hopes. Away pressure is extreme.
